rulings.Case Title: DAVID WRIGHT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Brand Insulation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES DENHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Dynalectric Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. McKenna¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Denham. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that its employees were handling and/or disturbing products that contained asbestos. Since plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue on that topic, whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos is irrelevant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES DENHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 2, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant FDCC California Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (strict liability and false representation). As to issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support their claim for punitive damages. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery asking for all of plaintiffs¿ evidence supporting their claim for punitive damages and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit affirmative evidence that negates an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claim. Defendant¿s status as a dissolved corporation does not preclude the imposition of punitive damages. As to issues 2 and 3, plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES DENHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Crum¿s deposition testimony and to Mr. Crosby¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GERALDINE HILT et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 3, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Anderson, Rowe & Buckley Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication: The motion is denied as to issue 1 (summary judgment) and issues 2 and 3 (prior dismissal). Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Hilt was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to comprehensive contention discovery in this case. The court did not consider the discovery from the personal injury action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claim. As to the dismissal in the personal injury action, defendant failed to demonstrate that either (1) there was a public and formal indication by the trial court of the legal merits of the case, or (2) there was some procedural dereliction by the plaintiff that made dismissal otherwise inevitable. (See, e.g., Franklin Capital Corp. v. Wilson (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 187, 200; Cravens v. State Board of Equalization (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 253; Zapanta v. Universal Care (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1167.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
